title: Merck — Oncology — Latest Briefing
html:
What Changed in Merck Oncology — Q1 2026

Generated: 2026-04-24 Quarter covered: January - March 2026 Data sources: Merck Q1 2026 earnings expected April 30, 2026 (not yet reported); FDA actions Q1 2026; ZoomRx study deliverables; analyst estimates Reading time: ~12 minutes

1. TL;DR (5 Bullets)

  1. Merck Q1 2026 earnings not yet reported — scheduled for April 30, 2026. Consensus estimate for Keytruda is ~$7.72B. This document will be updated once actuals are available.
  2. Ovarian cancer approved in February 2026 — FDA approved KEYTRUDA and KEYTRUDA QLEX + paclitaxel +/- bevacizumab for PD-L1+ (CPS >=1) platinum-resistant epithelial ovarian, fallopian tube, or primary peritoneal carcinoma. First and only PD-1 inhibitor in this setting. This is a significant new indication that reinforces the women's cancers growth vector.
  3. Permanent QLEX J-code (J9277) effective April 1, 2026 — this is the single most important catalyst for QLEX adoption. The temporary J9999 code was suppressing uptake due to billing uncertainty and reimbursement delays. April marks the beginning of the real QLEX adoption curve.
  4. KN-689 is approximately 10 months post-launch — qualitative signals from ZoomRx listening sessions (November 2025, January 2026, April 2026) show continued positive HCP experience, but reflexive PD-L1 testing is still not universal and community adoption lags.
  5. Padcev + Keytruda approved perioperatively for cisplatin-ineligible MIBC — expanding Keytruda's perioperative footprint into bladder cancer, further validating the TAM expansion strategy.

2. Data Events That Matter

FDA Approval: Ovarian Cancer — Keytruda's First Ovarian Nod

  • What happened: In February 2026, FDA approved pembrolizumab (KEYTRUDA and KEYTRUDA QLEX) in combination with paclitaxel, with or without bevacizumab, for adults with PD-L1+ (CPS >=1) platinum-resistant epithelial ovarian, fallopian tube, or primary peritoneal carcinoma as second or third-line treatment.
  • What it means: Ovarian cancer has ~22,000 new cases annually in the US. Platinum-resistant disease is a high unmet-need setting with poor outcomes. This is the first PD-1 approval in ovarian cancer, opening a patient population that has historically had limited immunotherapy options. Notably, QLEX was included in the label from day one — meaning every new ovarian patient is a potential QLEX adoption opportunity.
  • What Vinoth should say: "The ovarian cancer approval is strategically important on two dimensions. First, it adds a new revenue stream — platinum-resistant ovarian is a real unmet need. Second, it's the first indication where QLEX is available from the very start, with no IV-to-SC transition needed. This is the cleanest test case for QLEX-native prescribing."

QLEX J-Code Transition: April 1, 2026

  • What happened: The permanent HCPCS code J9277 (pembrolizumab + berahyaluronidase alfa-pmph, 1mg) became effective April 1, 2026, replacing the temporary J9999.
  • What it means: This removes the single largest adoption friction. With J9999, oncology practices faced billing uncertainty — some payers were slow to process claims, creating accounts receivable risk. J9277 establishes clear coding, predictable reimbursement, and integration into existing billing workflows. Historical precedent (Rituxan SC, Darzalex Faspro) shows that permanent J-code assignment accelerates adoption significantly.
  • What Vinoth should say: "April 1 is the real QLEX launch date. Everything before was soft launch. If we see meaningful QLEX revenue step-up in Q2 2026, the LOE defense thesis strengthens materially. Our SubQ IO study conversations from April onward are the most valuable data we'll collect."

Padcev + Keytruda Perioperative Bladder Cancer

  • What happened: FDA approved pembrolizumab (KEYTRUDA and KEYTRUDA QLEX) with enfortumab vedotin (Padcev) for perioperative treatment of cisplatin-ineligible muscle-invasive bladder cancer (MIBC).
  • What it means: This is the third perioperative indication for Keytruda (after NSCLC KN-671 and HNSCC KN-689), further validating the TAM expansion strategy. Cisplatin-ineligible MIBC is a specific niche but the combination with Padcev positions Keytruda at the center of the emerging bladder cancer IO paradigm.

ZoomRx Study Updates (Q1 2026)

  • KN-689 Final Report v4 delivered (Pre-read): The comprehensive final report covering Aug 2025-Feb 2026 is complete. This includes 7 HCP-Pt conversations and 14 HCP qualitative interviews with detailed findings on PD-L1 testing, MDT dynamics, and KN-689 post-launch perceptions.
  • KN-689 April 2026 Listening Session (v1.0): New wave of listening sessions in field. This represents the most current post-launch signal.
  • SubQ IO study continuing recruitment: 7 conversations as of November 2025 kickoff deck. Study spans NSCLC, melanoma, TNBC, RCC, and urothelial across neoadjuvant, adjuvant, and metastatic settings. Final report delivery expected Q1-Q2 2026.

3. Competitor Earnings Intelligence

Note: Merck Q1 2026 earnings are scheduled for April 30, 2026 — not yet reported. Keytruda consensus estimate is ~$7.72B. Competitor data below reflects reported Q1 2026 earnings where available.

Revenue Snapshot — Q1 2026

Company Brand Q1 2026 Revenue YoY Growth Key Signal
BMS Opdivo ~$2.3B +4% Seasonal softness; managing through LOE preparation; biosimilar entry expected 2027-2028
AstraZeneca Imfinzi ~$1.1B (seasonal) +12% POSEIDON showing promotional fatigue; AEGEAN perioperative building
AstraZeneca DATROWAY ~$80M N/A (ramp) Accelerating in mBC; TROPION-Lung Phase 3 NSCLC ongoing
Roche Tecentriq ~$800M -12% Effectively in managed decline; Roche focus shifting to ADC/bispecific platforms
Keytruda ~$7.7B (est.) TBD Pending Apr 30; seasonal Q1 softness expected; J-code transition April 1

BMS — Opdivo (~$2.3B, +4%)

BMS Q1 2026 call (April 24, 2026):

  1. Opdivo growth decelerating as biosimilar preparation intensifies: Q1 growth of 4% (vs. 6% in Q4) reflects both seasonal softness and the broader reality that Opdivo is approaching the LOE preparation phase. BMS guided that nivolumab biosimilar entry remains on track for 2027-2028, and management is openly discussing the transition strategy.

  2. Perioperative NSCLC remains BMS's growth narrative, but Keytruda maintains first-mover advantage: BMS continued to promote CheckMate-77T perioperative data, but acknowledged that Keytruda's earlier approval (KN-671, July 2024) and HNSCC expansion (KN-689, June 2025) have established Merck's perioperative IO franchise as the broader platform.

  3. Subcutaneous Opdivo in development — following Merck's QLEX playbook: BMS disclosed that SC nivolumab formulations are in clinical development, explicitly referencing the QLEX approval as validation of the SC IO approach. BMS is ~18-24 months behind Merck on SC formulation.

  4. BMS's IO strategy is shifting from volume to combination value: Management emphasized Opdualag as the combination extension and LAG-3 as a differentiation pathway. BMS is pivoting from "more Opdivo indications" to "higher-value Opdivo combinations" — a quality-over-quantity defense.

Implication for Merck: BMS's SC Opdivo development validates QLEX's strategic importance — if the competitor is copying your LOE defense, you're doing it right. BMS being 18-24 months behind on SC gives Merck a critical head start in establishing subcutaneous IO as the new standard of care. The Q2 2026 QLEX adoption data (first full quarter under permanent J-code) will be the defining datapoint.

Roche — Tecentriq (~$800M, -12%)

Roche Q1 2026 call:

  1. Tecentriq's decline accelerated to -12% YoY: The trajectory has worsened each quarter (Q3 -8%, Q4 -10%, Q1 -12%), confirming that Tecentriq is in irreversible decline. Roche's FY2026 guidance no longer highlights Tecentriq as a growth asset.

  2. Roche pivoting IO investment entirely to next-generation platforms: Management explicitly stated that future oncology investment is directed at ADCs (polatuzumab vedotin partnerships), bispecific T-cell engagers (glofitamab, mosunetuzumab), and novel combinations. Tecentriq is the IO backbone for combinations but no longer receives standalone promotional investment.

  3. Tiragolumab (anti-TIGIT) program continues but with tempered expectations: After mixed Phase 3 results, Roche is pursuing tiragolumab in select combinations rather than broad expansion. The anti-TIGIT class has not delivered the hoped-for IO reinvigoration.

Implication for Merck: Roche's complete pivot away from IO competition confirms the two-player IO structure (Keytruda + Opdivo). However, Roche's ADC and bispecific investments are the longer-term watch — if ADC + IO combinations require a different PD-1 backbone (e.g., partnered with Roche's ADCs), Merck must ensure Keytruda/QLEX is the partner of choice.

AstraZeneca — Imfinzi ($1.1B, +12%) + DATROWAY ($80M)

AZ Q1 2026 call:

  1. Imfinzi growth moderating to 12% (from 18% in Q4): Seasonal softness partially explains the deceleration, but AZ also noted "promotional fatigue" with the POSEIDON regimen (Imfinzi + tremelimumab + chemo in metastatic NSCLC). PACIFIC-based Stage III NSCLC remains the anchor.

  2. AEGEAN perioperative NSCLC is AZ's next growth catalyst, but regulatory timeline uncertain: AZ is pursuing perioperative durvalumab in resectable NSCLC (AEGEAN), but no approval date has been confirmed. This leaves AZ behind Merck (KN-671 approved July 2024) and BMS (CheckMate-77T data mature) in perioperative NSCLC.

  3. DATROWAY accelerating to ~$80M in Q1 — the ADC threat is materializing: DATROWAY's ramp in HR+/HER2- mBC is outpacing initial expectations. AZ also disclosed that TROPION-Lung Phase 3 data (datopotamab deruxtecan in NSCLC) is ongoing, which would bring an ADC directly into Keytruda's core lung cancer territory.

  4. AZ framing the future as "IO + ADC" rather than "IO vs. IO": Management positioned the combination of Imfinzi + DATROWAY (and future ADCs) as the next-generation regimen, explicitly stating that "the PD-1/PD-L1 monotherapy era is maturing but IO remains the backbone of combination therapy."

Implication for Merck: AZ's TROPION-Lung program is the most important competitive catalyst to watch at ASCO 2026. If datopotamab deruxtecan shows activity in NSCLC, the IO + ADC paradigm enters lung cancer — and the question becomes which IO backbone (Keytruda vs. Imfinzi vs. Opdivo) pairs best with ADCs. Merck must ensure Keytruda/QLEX is the preferred IO partner in ADC combination trials.

Synthesis: What the Combined Q1 2026 Picture Tells Us

  1. Keytruda's market position remains unassailable in the near term, but the competitive conversation is shifting from IO share to IO's role in combinations. All three competitors (BMS, Roche, AZ) are discussing next-generation combinations (IO + ADC, IO + bispecific, IO + LAG-3) rather than challenging Keytruda's IO monotherapy/doublet dominance.

  2. The LOE preparation race is real and Merck is ahead. BMS explicitly referenced QLEX as validation for their own SC Opdivo development. Merck's 18-24 month head start in SC IO is a genuine competitive moat. The QLEX J-code transition (April 1) makes this the quarter where SC adoption becomes measurable.

  3. DATROWAY's acceleration and TROPION-Lung bring the ADC threat closer to Keytruda's core indications. At ~$80M quarterly (annualized >$300M), DATROWAY is no longer a rounding error. If the NSCLC ADC data reads out positively, Keytruda's response must be "combine with us" rather than "compete against us."

  4. Tecentriq's -12% decline effectively removes Roche from IO competition. The IO market is now Keytruda (dominant), Opdivo (mature #2), Imfinzi (growing niche), and everyone else declining or immaterial. This simplifies Merck's competitive monitoring to two companies: BMS and AZ.

4. Commercial Pulse

Metric Q4 2025 (actual) Q1 2026 (estimate) Commentary
Keytruda Revenue $8.4B ~$7.7B (consensus) Seasonal Q1 softness typical; lower than Q4
QLEX Revenue $35M TBD (J-code effect begins April 1) Q1 still under J9999; April+ under J9277
YoY Growth +5% TBD Consensus implies continued mid-single-digit
Key Q1 Event — Ovarian approval, Padcev perioperative, J-code transition —

Critical forward metric: Q2 2026 QLEX revenue. This will be the first full quarter under the permanent J-code and the true adoption signal. If QLEX reaches $100M+ in Q2, the 30-40% transition target by 2028 becomes credible.

5. Thesis Update

Thesis Status Key Evidence This Quarter
1. QLEX adoption determines post-2028 floor APPROACHING INFLECTION J-code effective April 1, 2026. The next 90 days determine trajectory. Ovarian cancer is the first QLEX-native indication.
2. KN-689 slow burn — surgeons are bottleneck UNCHANGED Listening sessions continue to show positive but cautious adoption. No quantitative W2 data yet.
3. Perioperative TAM expansion is right bet STRENGTHENED Third perioperative indication (Padcev+Keytruda in MIBC) added. Three tumor types now have perioperative Keytruda.
4. MDT is the intervention point UNCHANGED KN-689 final report delivered — detailed MDT dynamics mapped. Awaiting quantitative validation.
5. Three-pronged LOE defense PROGRESSING QLEX hits J-code milestone. Perioperative expands to bladder. Ovarian adds new indication. All three defense vectors advancing.

Net assessment: Q1 2026 is a setup quarter. The earnings haven't reported yet, the J-code just turned on, and ovarian just launched. The real story is written in Q2-Q3 2026 — specifically, whether QLEX adoption inflects and whether KN-689 shows quantitative uptake.

6. What to Watch Next Quarter (Q2 2026)

  1. Merck Q1 2026 earnings (April 30, 2026) — Keytruda number, QLEX breakout (if provided), management commentary on J-code impact and ovarian launch trajectory.
  2. QLEX Q2 2026 revenue — the defining datapoint. Must show meaningful step-change from Q1. Target: $80-120M would signal real traction.
  3. ASCO 2026 (late May/June) — watch for: KN-689 updated data (longer follow-up), any new Keytruda perioperative data, competitive IO data (Opdivo combinations, Imfinzi updates). Abstract releases in late May.
  4. SubQ IO study report delivery — with 30 target conversations, the study should have sufficient data for pattern detection by mid-2026. First real-world QLEX conversation analysis.
  5. KN-689 ATU W2 results — first quantitative post-launch adoption measurement. If fielded Aug 2025, results should be available by now. This is the missing data point in our adoption narrative.
  6. IRA drug price negotiation — Keytruda selected for price negotiation under the Inflation Reduction Act. Negotiated price effective 2028 (same year as biosimilar entry). The interaction between negotiated price and biosimilar dynamics is uncertain and commercially significant.

7. Conversation Starters for Merck

  1. "Three perioperative indications in 24 months — NSCLC, HNSCC, bladder. You're building a perioperative IO franchise, and ZoomRx has the only systematic measurement of how that franchise is being adopted across tumor types. The surgeon referral patterns, MDT dynamics, and biomarker testing workflows are remarkably consistent across indications. Let us build a cross-indication perioperative tracker." — Pitches the portfolio play, not a one-off study.

  2. "The ovarian approval is the first QLEX-native launch — no IV-to-SC transition, just pure new-start prescribing. If we track QLEX conversation dynamics in ovarian specifically, we can separate the 'formulation preference' signal from the 'physician inertia' noise. That's the data your LOE defense team needs." — Creates a targeted, high-value QLEX study concept.

  3. "Your Q1 2026 earnings call will be the first time you're asked about the J-code impact. We'll have SubQ IO conversation data from April onward — the same window the Street is asking about. Let us brief you before your investor call with real-world evidence of how the J-code is changing clinic conversations." — Time-bound, high-impact positioning.

8. Appendix: Upcoming Merck Oncology Catalysts (Q2-Q3 2026)

Date Event Relevance
April 30, 2026 Merck Q1 2026 earnings Keytruda revenue, QLEX update, FY guidance confirmation
Late May 2026 ASCO abstract releases KN-689 updates, competitive IO data, ADC readouts
June 2026 ASCO annual meeting Keytruda data across indications, competitive landscape
Q2 2026 SubQ IO study report First real-world QLEX conversation analysis
Q2-Q3 2026 KN-689 ATU W2 results First quantitative post-launch adoption data
Q3 2026 Merck Q2 2026 earnings Critical QLEX adoption signal (first full J-code quarter)

Back to TA Knowledge Hub | Home

SubQ IO study report delivery — with 30 target conversations, the study should have sufficient data for pattern detection by mid-2026. First real-world QLEX conversation analysis.
KN-689 ATU W2 results — first quantitative post-launch adoption measurement. If fielded Aug 2025, results should be available by now. This is the missing data point in our adoption narrative.
IRA drug price negotiation — Keytruda selected for price negotiation under the Inflation Reduction Act. Negotiated price effective 2028 (same year as biosimilar entry). The interaction between negotiated price and biosimilar dynamics is uncertain and commercially significant.


7. Conversation Starters for Merck


"Three perioperative indications in 24 months — NSCLC, HNSCC, bladder. You're building a perioperative IO franchise, and ZoomRx has the only systematic measurement of how that franchise is being adopted across tumor types. The surgeon referral patterns, MDT dynamics, and biomarker testing workflows are remarkably consistent across indications. Let us build a cross-indication perioperative tracker." — Pitches the portfolio play, not a one-off study.


"The ovarian approval is the first QLEX-native launch — no IV-to-SC transition, just pure new-start prescribing. If we track QLEX conversation dynamics in ovarian specifically, we can separate the 'formulation preference' signal from the 'physician inertia' noise. That's the data your LOE defense team needs." — Creates a targeted, high-value QLEX study concept.


"Your Q1 2026 earnings call will be the first time you're asked about the J-code impact. We'll have SubQ IO conversation data from April onward — the same window the Street is asking about. Let us brief you before your investor call with real-world evidence of how the J-code is changing clinic conversations." — Time-bound, high-impact positioning.
